title: crataegus intricata var. biltmoreana
intro: Crataegus intricata var. biltmoreana, also known as Biltmore hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn plant species native to the southeastern United States. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts and leaves have been used to improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure, cholesterol levels, and triglycerides, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, are made from the plant's leaves, flowers, and bark to harness its health benefits.
